mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month increase in the unemployment rate signals incipient labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If a 3-month rise in UNRATE is followed by positive SPX 5-day returns more than 60% of the time, or if the Rank IC of the trend signal is not significantly negative at the 5% level over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate rises above 0.50%, it signals the end of the zero-lower-bound era and the start of monetary policy normalization, which increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If after triggering, the median 60-day SPX return is not negative, or if the signal has a positive Rank IC over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in both retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) confirm broad-based demand strength that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and INDPRO show positive MoM growth
failure_condition: If the joint positive MoM signal yields negative median 20-day forward returns, or if the signal's hit rate is below 50% in at least 30 out of 60 trailing months.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation regime (CPI YoY below 2%), an increase in the CPI signals healthy demand without triggering immediate monetary tightening, which supports equity prices. Conversely, in a high inflation regime, rising CPI forces Fed action and hurts equities. At the decision time, inflation is low, so the mechanism predicts positive returns from rising CPI.
failure_condition: If in periods with CPI YoY below 2%, a positive monthly change in CPIAUCSL leads to negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal fails to produce a positive Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window when the trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent upward trend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding output capacity and economic activity, which translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If a 3-month rolling increase in INDPRO is followed by negative SPX 5-day returns more than 50% of the time, or if the Rank IC over a 5-year period is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1,200 thousand units, it signals robust residential investment and consumer confidence, leading to positive spillovers to equities over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If after triggering, the median 60-day SPX return is not positive, or if the trigger occurs fewer than 10 times in the walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP (GDPC1) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a self-reinforcing expansion where employment gains boost income and spending, supporting a sustained equity rally over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: both GDPC1 and PAYEMS show positive QoQ or MoM growth
failure_condition: If the joint positive growth signal yields negative median 60-day forward returns, or if the signal's hit rate is below 55% in expansions as defined by NBER.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is already low (below 5.0%), further increases in payrolls (PAYEMS) exacerbate labor market tightness, leading to wage inflation and margin compression. This late-cycle dynamic produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If in periods with UNRATE below 5%, positive PAYEMS growth is followed by positive median SPX 20-day returns, or if the signal's negative Rank IC is not statistically significant over a 5-year rolling window.
